src/db/entitlementStore.js (usage_records has no unique constraint on (org, plan_key, usage_key, period_start) to upsert against -- fetch-then-write pattern used instead, same as every other store; a real concurrency guard would need a migration to add that constraint, not attempted this session)
knownRisks: Only 5 of 28 persistence-layer functions have been live-smoke-tested (F001, F005, F008, F019, F023) -- everything else is schema-verified but not individually exercised live, except the entitlement_limits seed (verified live via direct SELECT, not through the full application stack).
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled
No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- 001_initial_schema.sql and this session's entitlement-limit seed were both run via one-off root-level scripts, deleted after use

The live database now contains smoke-test data (one organization named 'Smoke Test Org', one membership, one audit event, one ticket) plus 5 real entitlement_limits rows -- worth knowing it's a mix of test and real data, not assuming the database is empty or purely synthetic

usage_records has no DB-level uniqueness constraint on (organization_id, plan_key, usage_key, period_start) -- entitlementStore.recordUsage() relies on fetch-then-write, same pattern as every other store in this codebase, but this one is more exposed to a lost-update race under real concurrent requests than most; worth a follow-up migration once real traffic exists
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)

Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; new care_hub_documents/file_assets Postgres tables use an opaque storageRef instead, but the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js remains Blobs-backed (F056 stayed on Blobs by design)
CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review

No Netlify Function HTTP endpoints exist yet for any Care Hub feature -- everything built so far is domain/policy/persistence layer only, callable from Node but not yet reachable over HTTP or from a browser
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY, but the two largest remaining owner decisions (data store, pricing/plan-limits) are now both resolved and built out end to end. 41 functions have real tested logic, 28 with real Postgres persistence, 389 passing unit tests, live database verification from Session 13, and real plan-limit data now seeded live. Remaining work: Netlify Function HTTP endpoints (nothing is exposed via HTTP yet), a UI, and 7 remaining owner decisions (registration model, retention/legal, new providers, remote-support scope, AI policy, missing workbooks).
